result.name: Biology Minor
result.templateAssets:
[{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

UC Santa Cruz students may complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and credit limits. Minors are recorded on the official transcript but do not appear on the diploma. Pursuing a minor is optional and should align with a student’s academic interests and career goals.


Students do not apply for minors as part of their UC Santa Cruz admission application. Transfer students interested in a minor should reach out to the department that offers it for advising during their first quarter of enrollment.

Minor","groupId":"31786f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Transfer students interested in this minor are encouraged to complete the articulated equivalents of the courses listed in the \"Preparation for the Minor\" section below before transferring.


NOTE: BIOL 20L is not required for students who complete the articulated equivalents of BIOL 20A and BIOE 20B at California community colleges. Refer to \"Preparation for the Minor,\" Group #1 below for courses approved to satisfy these requirements.


It is strongly recommended that students complete all of their required General Chemistry courses at a single institution.



Visit the Molecular, Cell and Developmental Biology (MCD) website to learn more about this minor.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Biology Minor Program Statement
